sensitivity: Standalone: 74.1% (bitewing), 72.9% (periapical); Clinical reader unassisted vs assisted: 74.9% to 84.0% (bitewing), 74.7% to 84.4% (periapical)
specificity: Standalone: 99.4% (bitewing), 99.6% (periapical); Clinical reader unassisted vs assisted: 98.8% to 98.6% (bitewing), 98.1% to 98.0% (periapical)
auc: Standalone AFROC: 0.859 (bitewing), 0.867 (periapical); Clinical reader AFROC unassisted vs assisted: 0.840 to 0.878 (bitewing), 0.846 to 0.900 (periapical)
intended_use: Overjet Calculus Assist (OCalA) is a radiological automated concurrent-read computer-assisted detection software intended to aid in the detection of interproximal calculus deposits on both bitewing an
intended_use_full: Overjet Calculus Assist (OCalA) is a radiological automated concurrent-read computer-assisted detection software intended to aid in the detection of interproximal calculus deposits on both bitewing and periapical radiographs. The Overjet Calculus Assist surrounds suspected calculus deposits with a bounding box. The device provides additional information for the dentist to use in their diagnosis of a tooth surface suspected of containing calculus deposits. The device is not intended as a replacement for a complete dentist's review or their clinical judgment that takes into account other relevant information from the image or patient history. The system is to be used by professionally trained and licensed dentists.
ground_truth: Consensus labels from three US-licensed dentists; non-consensus labels adjudicated by an oral radiologist
other_metrics: Reader improvement demonstrated with statistically significant increases in AFROC AUC for both bitewing (p=0.0055) and periapical (p=1.47e-05) radiographs when using device assistance
